title: 9 Herbal Creams For Upper Abdominal Pain
intro_desc: If you're experiencing upper abdominal pain, herbal creams might be worth trying. These creams contain active ingredients from plants that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the pain. But how do herbal teas come in? They work in a different way. When you drink herbal teas, the active compounds are absorbed into your bloodstream, where they can help calm inflammation and relax your digestive system. This can bring relief from upper abdominal pain.  For example, Curcuma longa (turmeric) has a powerful compound called curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. Zingiber officinale (ginger) also contains gingerols, which can help ease digestive discomfort and reduce inflammation. Another example is Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root), which has anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe stomach ulcers and reduce pain.  When you use herbal teas to relieve upper abdominal pain, you can experience a range of benefits. Your digestive system will be calmer, which means you'll be less likely to experience discomfort, bloating, or cramps. You may also find that your energy levels increase, as your body is no longer working to manage pain. Additionally, herbal teas can help you relax, which can improve your mood and reduce stress.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for upper abdominal pain

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains a compound called curcumin, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. Curcumin has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The bioactive constituents of Curcuma longa, including curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, have been shown to inhibit the production of inflammatory enzymes and molecules that cause pain. These compounds also have a protective effect on the digestive system, which can help alleviate upper abdominal pain caused by digestive issues. By reducing inflammation and promoting digestive health, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of shea butter, and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in the double boiler until they are completely melted.
Add 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder (Curcuma longa) and 2 tablespoons of lavender oil to the melted mixture.
Stir the mixture well and remove it from the heat. Let it cool for 10-15 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once cooled, it's ready to use as a cream for upper abdominal pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains gingerols and shogaols, bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the upper abdominal area, which can cause pain. Gingerols and shogaols also have a warming effect, which can help to increase blood flow and relaxation of the muscles in the abdominal region. This increased blood flow and relaxation of muscles can help to alleviate pain caused by cramps, spasms, or digestive issues. The analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties of gingerols and shogaols in Zingiber officinale creams can help to provide relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 10 tablespoons of Zingiber officinale (fresh ginger) root, and 10 drops of peppermint essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Grate the Zingiber officinale root and add it to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes. Stir in the peppermint essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely. Once set, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount and apply it to the upper abdominal area as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and discomfort in the upper abdomen. Glycyrrhizin also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, calming irritation and inflammation in the stomach and intestines. The licorice root extract in Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains flavonoids and saponins, which work together to protect the stomach lining from damage and promote healing. Additionally, glycyrrhizin helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, easing cramps and spasms that can cause upper abdominal pain. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide relief from upper abdominal pain caused by digestive issues.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Glycyrrhiza glabra extract, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in Glycyrrhiza glabra extract, shea butter, and vitamin E oil until well combined.
Pour mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, apply a small amount of cream to the affected area of the upper abdomen as needed to help soothe and relieve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains boiactive constituents like linalool, linalyl acetate, and camphor. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the upper abdominal area. The antispasmodic properties, in particular, can help reduce muscle cramps and spasms that may be contributing to the pain. Additionally, the calming effects of linalool and linalyl acetate can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate upper abdominal pain. By applying Lavandula angustifolia creams topically, the active constituents can penetrate the skin and provide localized relief from inflammation and pain.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Steep 1/4 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in 1 cup of distilled water for 30 minutes. Strain the mixture and set aside.
Melt 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the steeped Lavandula angustifolia mixture, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 1-2 drops of lavender essential oil (optional) to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools. Pour into a container and let it set at room temperature. Use as needed for upper abdominal pain relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams contains a compound called quinine, which has been used to help alleviate upper abdominal pain. Quinine has anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and pain in the abdominal area. The alkaloids present in Cinchona officinalis, including quinine, cinchonine, and cinchonidine, have a spasmolytic effect, meaning they can relax the muscles and prevent cramping. These properties can be beneficial in treating conditions such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and menstrual cramps, which often cause upper abdominal pain. The anti-inflammatory and spasmolytic properties of Cinchona officinalis creams make it a potential remedy for upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Cinchona officinalis herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of Cinchona officinalis bark powder, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/4 cup of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the Cinchona officinalis bark powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes, or until it reaches a temperature that's comfortable to apply to the skin.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool completely before applying it to the upper abdominal area to relieve pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, a key bioactive constituent responsible for its medicinal properties. Rosmarinus officinalis creams work by reducing inflammation in the upper abdominal area, which can contribute to pain. The anti-inflammatory properties of rosmarinic acid help to decrease swelling and ease discomfort. Additionally, the compound also has antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the abdominal region, further reducing pain. The reduction in inflammation and muscle relaxation provided by Rosmarinus officinalis creams may help alleviate upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Add shea butter and stir until melted. Remove from heat and let cool slightly.
Add rosemary essential oil and jojoba oil to the mixture. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let cool completely. Once cooled, apply a small amount to the affected area to help soothe upper abdominal pain.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthol, menthone, and limonene as its active constituents. These compounds have a numbing effect on the skin and mucous membranes, which can help reduce pain in the upper abdominal area. Menthol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe irritated tissues and reduce swelling. Additionally, menthone has a relaxing effect on the digestive system, which can help calm cramps and spasms in the stomach. By applying mentha x piperita creams topically, the active constituents can work directly on the affected area to provide relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil, and a small saucepan.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in the saucepan over low heat, stirring occasionally.
Once melted, remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool slightly.
Add 2 tablespoons of peppermint essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains the active constituent furanocoumarins, which have antispasmodic properties that help to relax the muscles in the upper abdominal area. The volatile oils in Foeniculum vulgare creams, particularly anethole, also have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms. The sesquiterpene compounds, such as bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe the digestive tract and reduce pain. Foeniculum vulgare creams also contain phenolic compounds, like umbelliferone, which have antioxidant properties that help to protect the digestive system from damage. The combination of these compounds in Foeniculum vulgare creams helps to reduce upper abdominal pain by relaxing muscles, reducing inflammation, and protecting the digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds (aniseed) and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
In a small saucepan, heat 1/2 cup of coconut oil over low heat.
Add 1/4 cup of dried Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the saucepan and let it steep for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into the glass bowl with the remaining coconut oil.
Mix in 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil into the mixture. Let it cool and solidify before applying to the affected area.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams contains silymarin as its active constituent, which has anti-inflammatory properties. Silymarin helps to reduce inflammation in the abdominal area, which can cause upper abdominal pain. The antioxidant properties of silymarin also help to protect the liver from damage, which can contribute to upper abdominal pain. Silymarin also has a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing irritation and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and soothing the digestive system, Silybum marianum creams may provide relief from upper abdominal pain.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal creams for upper abdominal pain
remedy_procedure: Gather 100g of dried Silybum marianum (also known as Milk Thistle) flowers.
Combine 1 cup of water and 100g of dried flowers in a saucepan. Heat on low for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter. Discard the solids.
Combine the liquid with 1/2 cup of coconut oil or other carrier oil in a saucepan. Heat on low until the oil is warm.
Let the mixture cool. Pour into a jar and store in a cool, dark place. Apply a small amount to the affected area as needed.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help soothe upper abdominal pain. Certain teas, like ginger and peppermint, contain compounds that can ease digestive issues. They can also calm the stomach and reduce inflammation. Some people find that these teas help alleviate discomfort and promote a sense of calm, which may in turn reduce upper abdominal pain.
faq_safe: While some herbal teas may help soothe upper abdominal pain, daily consumption isn't always a good idea. Peppermint tea, for example, can irritate stomach acid and make symptoms worse. Ginger tea, on the other hand, may help calm your stomach, but overdoing it can lead to stomach upset. Moderation is key.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can provide relief from upper abdominal pain in a few hours to a few days. For example, ginger tea can soothe the stomach and reduce discomfort within 30 minutes to an hour. Peppermint tea can ease bloating and cramping in 2-3 hours. Results may vary depending on the individual and the specific tea used.
faq_time: For upper abdominal pain, try drinking herbal tea in the evening. This allows your body to digest the tea and any other foods you've eaten throughout the day, helping to soothe your stomach.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with some prescription medications for upper abdominal pain. Certain herbs like St. John's Wort, garlic, and ginger can affect the way your body processes these medications. This is because they can either boost or weaken the effects of the medications, which can lead to unwanted side effects or make the medications less effective.
faq_pregnant: When you're pregnant and have upper abdominal pain, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs can cause problems during pregnancy, like stimulating the uterus or affecting blood flow. If you want to try herbal teas, look for ones specifically labeled as safe for pregnancy, like peppermint or chamomile.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for upper abdominal pain while breastfeeding, but some may not be safe. Certain herbs like peppermint and ginger can ease discomfort, but others like pennyroyal and blue cohosh can harm the baby. Always check the ingredients before consuming.
title_2: 9 Herbal Creams to Ease Upper Abdominal Pain in Elderly Individuals
